content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Found 2 correctness regressions in the new bead prompt parser: description-only acceptance criteria are dropped, and required subsections that follow `Non-goals:` are misclassified as non-goals. I did not find other concrete bugs in the contract plumbing after reading the updated prompt-generation and workflow-consumer files.

## Amendments

1. `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:618-627` now strips every line after a `## Acceptance Criteria` marker out of `Must-Do Scope`, but `src/cli/project.rs:332-333` only populates `bead_acceptance_criteria` from the structured field. Many existing beads still carry their acceptance criteria only in the description, for example `.beads/issues.jsonl:1` (`ralph-burning-0gn`). For those beads the generated prompt now says `No explicit acceptance criteria were supplied.` and silently drops the real acceptance criteria. Preserve description-sourced acceptance criteria when the structured field is empty, or stop deleting that section unless it has been materialized into `bead_acceptance_criteria`.
   Rationale: This is a direct scope regression: implementers and reviewers lose the acceptance boundary on existing beads, so downstream stages can approve incomplete work.
2. `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:618-627` leaves `active_section` set to `NonGoals` until `Acceptance Criteria`, so any later subsection is reclassified as out-of-scope. Existing beads already use `Non-goals:` followed by required `Contract hardening notes:` blocks, for example `.beads/issues.jsonl:17` and `.beads/issues.jsonl:30`. The new renderer will move those hardening notes into `## Explicit Non-Goals`, telling downstream agents not to do required work. Reset the parser when a new non-non-goal subsection starts, or parse only the explicit non-goal bullets instead of treating the whole tail of the description as non-goals.
   Rationale: This mis-scopes live backlog items by turning mandatory requirements into explicit non-goals, which can drive wrong implementation and review decisions.
